description_long: This fire hydrant installation features a prominent red metal control box mounted on a concrete or gravel pad, set into a square section of earth. A set of exposed metallic pipes, including a valve and a wheel handle, extends from the side of the box, providing the main water connection and control mechanism. The pipes are robust and industrial, with visible joints and a metallic sheen, suggesting durability and resistance to weather. The control box has a warning label and a utility marking, indicating its emergency function and compliance with safety standards. The base is a textured, earthy platform, simulating a typical urban or roadside installation. The overall construction is solid and utilitarian, designed for easy access and operation by emergency personnel.
description: A red metal fire hydrant control box with exposed metallic pipes and a valve, mounted on a gravel pad set in earth. The setup is robust and functional, with clear emergency markings.
description_view_0: Front view showing the red control box, metallic pipes, valve, and wheel handle, all mounted on a gravel pad within an earthy base.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the narrow profile of the control box and the gravel pad, with minimal pipework visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the control box and the pipe assembly, with the valve and wheel handle visible from the side.
description_view_3: Opposite side view focusing on the pipework and valve assembly, with the control box seen edge-on.
